title: ARPA-H Awards $144M to 7 Teams for PROSPR Aging Research Initiative
description: ARPA-H's PROSPR program awards $144M to Stanford, UT San Antonio, Columbia, and four other teams to pioneer in-home clinical trials testing rapamycin, GLP-1 agonists, and SGLT2 inhibitors for aging.

title: First Phase 3 Clinical Trial Targeting Aging Launched via PROSPR
description: ARPA-H partners with FDA to run the first Phase 3 trial targeting aging in healthy adults, evaluating rapamycin, GLP-1 agonists, and SGLT2 inhibitors with a goal of a $100 home testing kit by 2031.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Reprogramming Trial, CAR-T Alzheimer's
description: Life Biosciences receives FDA clearance for first human cellular reprogramming trial targeting age-related vision diseases. Also covers CAR-T cells for Alzheimer's plaques and reprogramming for memory rescue.

title: 10 Breakthroughs That Changed Longevity in 2025
description: Sleep outranks diet and exercise as a longevity predictor, movement diversity emerges as the most accessible intervention, and senolytic randomized controlled trials advance significantly.

title: Top 10 Longevity & Anti-Aging Breakthroughs of 2025
description: Rapamycin confirmed as most reliable lifespan extender in 20 years of NIA ITP data. PEARL trial results, GLP-1 as longevity drug, and SGLT2 telomere lengthening among key breakthroughs.

title: Visceral Fat Linked to 44% Higher Cancer Risk — Rhonda Patrick
description: Dr. Rhonda Patrick highlights that visceral fat raises cancer risk by up to 44% and doubles early mortality risk. Fasting and HIIT are the most effective interventions for reducing it.

title: HIIT Is the Most Powerful Way to Improve Endothelial Function
description: Meta-analysis finds HIIT improves flow-mediated dilation by ~4%, meaningfully more than moderate cardio or resistance training, delivering a potent vascular training stimulus against cardiovascular disease.

title: Tyrosine Linked to Reduced Longevity in UK Biobank Study
description: Large UK Biobank study finds the amino acid tyrosine is associated with reduced longevity, with stronger effects observed in men.

title: AI-Driven Supercomputing Comes to Pharma for Longevity Research
description: AI-powered supercomputing enters pharmaceutical development, promising shorter drug timelines and new opportunities for longevity-focused drug discovery.

title: Brain Blood Flow Wearable Backed by Former Zomato CEO
description: A temple-worn wearable that tracks brain blood flow receives backing, signaling a shift from food tech to human performance and neurotech.

title: Centenarians' Blood Proteins Resemble Those of Much Younger Adults
description: First-of-its-kind Swiss proteomics study finds proteins in centenarians' blood closely resemble those of much younger adults, offering new biomarker insights.

title: Juvenescence Completes Phase 1 for First-in-Class PAI-1 Inhibitor
description: Juvenescence advances its first-in-class PAI-1 inhibitor through Phase 1, targeting key aging mechanisms and fibrotic disease pathways.

title: Gut Protein Solubility Declines With Age — New Research Funded
description: ResearchHub funds a study testing whether gut proteins become less soluble with aging, potentially linking immune metabolic remodeling to region-specific proteostasis instability.

title: ERG Phase Separation Attenuates Cellular Senescence
description: New research shows the ERG transcription factor reduces cellular senescence through phase separation mechanisms, with insights from centenarian immune cell chromatin accessibility.

title: Dietary Restriction Mechanisms Reviewed Across Species
description: Comprehensive review examines molecular mechanisms behind dietary restriction's lifespan benefits, including nutrient-sensing pathways and autophagy across multiple species.

title: Senolytic Mitochondrial Determinants of Efficacy and Resistance
description: Comparative analysis reveals mitochondrial quality control as a key determinant of resistance to senolytic drugs ABT263 and ARV825, providing a framework for combination senotherapies.

title: Machine Learning Senolytic Predictor Identifies New Drug Candidates
description: ML-based senolytic predictor identifies panaxatriol as a new senolytic agent and voclosporin as a lifespan extender in C. elegans, outperforming metformin.

title: Biohacking Goes Mainstream: Longevity Economy to Reach $63B by 2035
description: LinkedIn's Big Ideas 2026 report projects the longevity economy will reach $63 billion by 2035, nearly triple its 2024 value, as biohacking tools become accessible to consumers.

title: New Longevity Tech Boosts 100-Year Lifespan Prospects
description: Daily biometrics, smart scales, and AI companions are making it more likely that current and future generations will achieve a 100-year lifespan, per Milken Institute analysis.

title: Stanford THRIVE Coalition Secures $34.5M from ARPA-H
description: Stanford-led THRIVE coalition receives up to $34.5M from ARPA-H to build an FDA-grade intrinsic capacity score predicting 20-year health outcomes.

title: Nir Barzilai: 'Looking Young Doesn't Mean You're Healthy'
description: Gerontologist Nir Barzilai warns that biological age clocks can be misleading, arguing the practical question is turning average death at 80 into 35 healthy years rather than extended frailty.

title: NAD+ Revolution 2026: NMN, NR, and Latest Booster Research
description: Viral thread compiles 2025-2026 NAD+ supplement research including NMN vs NR comparisons, human trials, and brain health applications for Alzheimer's reversal models.

title: 5 Clinical Readouts to Watch in H1 2026
description: Key readouts include Eli Lilly's next-gen Alzheimer's drug remternetug and Novartis' cardiovascular candidate pelacarsen, both with major implications for aging-related disease.

title: Longevity Summit Dublin 2026 Opens Abstract Submissions
description: Longevity Summit Dublin 2026 opens poster abstract submissions for aging science research, taking place July 2-4 at Trinity College Dublin.

title: Rapamycin for Longevity: Comprehensive Pros, Cons, and Future Perspectives
description: Comprehensive review finds rapamycin extends mouse lifespan 9-14% from mid-life, but the PEARL trial shows only modest biomarker changes in humans. Timing and dosing remain critical open questions.
